---
title: "RP 32 Part 2: Daily Review and Reset"
format: html
---
# RP 32 Part 2: Daily Review and Reset

Recovery strengthens when daily planning is paired with daily review. Review helps you learn from real situations and prevents small slips from turning into larger setbacks.

Use review as feedback, not self-criticism. Ask: "What did I learn?" rather than "What's wrong with me?"

Daily review supports behavioral learning by turning experience into adjustment. Regular review helps prevent all-or-nothing thinking and keeps setbacks from becoming a longer slide away from recovery habits.

## Evening Review
| Question | My answer |
|---|---|
| What recovery action did I complete today? |  |
| What challenge came up? |  |
| How did I respond? |  |
| What will I adjust tomorrow? |  |

::: {.callout-tip}
### Skill rehearsal

Treat this as a rehearsal for moments when motivation is low and pressure is high.

Complete this review for five evenings in a row.

Repetition builds confidence and makes healthy responses easier to access.
:::
